title: Past Continuous
definition: The Past Continuous tense describes an action that was in progress at a specific time in the past, often interrupted by another action.

structure.positive: I was watching TV. / They were playing football.
structure.negative: I was not (wasn't) watching TV. / They were not (weren't) playing.
structure.question: Were you watching TV? / Was he playing?
structure.whQuestion: What were you doing at 8 PM? / Why was she crying?

usage: Use Past Continuous for an action in progress at a specific past moment (I was sleeping at midnight), an action interrupted by another (I was cooking when the phone rang), and two simultaneous past actions (While I was reading, she was cooking).
examples: I was sleeping when you called.
She was cooking dinner at 7 PM.
They were watching a movie last night.
While I was studying, my brother was playing games.
Was she working when the power went out?
What were you doing at midnight?

dailyConversation.speaker: A
dailyConversation.line: What were you doing when I called?

commonMistakes.why: State verbs are not used in continuous forms; use Past Simple instead.

commonMistakes.wrong: While I cooked, she was cleaning.
commonMistakes.right: While I was cooking, she was cleaning.
commonMistakes.why: Two simultaneous ongoing past actions both use Past Continuous.

quiz.question: They ___ (play) football when it started to rain.
Which word often signals Past Continuous?
quiz.explanation: 'While' introduces an action in progress, typical of Past Continuous.

quiz.sentence: She were watching TV at 9 PM.
quiz.correction: She was watching TV at 9 PM.
